rows.notes: Top-of-page title clearly written in larger script. No monetary columns present on this line; alignment with column rules is clear from following rows.
Section heading directly beneath the title. Text is legible and there are no amounts on this line.
Clear alignment of the pounds column; the entry shows '30 0 0' distinctly in the pounds/shillings/pence columns. Vertical ruling lines used to confirm columns.
Pounds '20' clearly written in the pounds column. Shillings and pence are both zero and aligned with the ruling lines.
Pounds '6' legible in the pounds column; shillings and pence columns blank/zero by alignment.
Reads as '7 10 0' with strong alignment to column lines. Shillings '10' clear and within 0–19 range.
Pounds '1' and shillings '10' readable; alignment with ruled columns consistent. Slight loop in script but digits unambiguous.
Amount appears as '3 6 0' in their respective columns. Handwriting clear and values valid.
Reads '1 10 0' though the descender strokes make the first digit slightly cramped. Column alignment supports this reading.
Pounds '2' and shillings '15' are clear. Ampersand-like hand for 'and' in description is legible. Values within valid ranges.
Pounds '9' and shillings '6' align well. Pence read as '8' but ink is slightly faint there; chosen '8' based on alignment with pence column and the shape of the digit (not 3 or 0).
Pounds '6' and shillings '13' clear. Pence appears as '4' though the loop is faint; column alignment supports '4'.
Pounds '1' is small but legible. Shillings '13' and pence '4' follow the same vertical alignment as prior lines; ink on pence is lighter but readable.
The place name 'Midenham' (spelled as on page) is clear. Amount '6 13 4' matches vertical alignment seen on adjacent rows; slight smearing but numbers consistent.
The line 'Little Tew.' appears as a sub-item (no visible amount in the monetary columns). Probably a descriptive sub-entry; no amounts are present or they are omitted here.
Amounts read as '2 12 2'. Pence digit is slightly compressed but reads as '2' by alignment with pence column.
Reads '2 6 0'; the pence column is blank/zero. 'Do.' indicates 'ditto' and is clearly written.

This line shows only a small figure in the pence column. The figure looks like '8' but is faint and could possibly be '3' or '0'; chosen '8' based on the stroke shape and spacing in the pence column. Low confidence.
Very faint single-digit in the shillings column, interpreted as '1'. The pounds column is blank. Ink degradation makes this uncertain.
Section header clearly written and separated from preceding entries. No monetary amount on this line.
Clear numeric trio '22 5 6' aligned with the columns. Ink contrast is good and values are unambiguous.
'4 19 0' is clearly visible; 'Do' (ditto) in the description is legible. All currency digits fall within valid ranges.
Clearly reads '20 0 0' in the money columns with strong alignment to the vertical rules.
Reads '4 4 0'. The two '4' digits are clear and placed in appropriate columns.
Amount '14 0 0' is clearly written and aligned. The entry text is legible.
Section header is clearly written and separated from monetary rows. No amounts present on this line.
Large pounds figure '134' is clear; shillings '5' and pence '9' align well with ruled columns. High contrast and legibility.
Reads as '172 14 0'. Parenthetical note in description is partially faint; numeric columns are clear though the '14' shillings is written tightly.
Large pounds '232' and shillings/pence '1 9' are clear and properly aligned. Ink contrast strong.

Description is partially abbreviated and parenthetical notes are faint; amount reads as '12 0 6'. There is moderate uncertainty about the exact letters in the parenthesis, but the numeric trio is aligned and legible.
Reads '124 0 3'; the pounds '124' is clear. 'Tenument' (spelled as on page) is legible though the ampersand is small.

Pounds and shillings read as '14 16'. Pence appears as '6' with a trailing loop interpreted as '3q' (three farthings) -> fraction 0.75. The fraction symbol is fainter than digits but has the characteristic loop indicating 3q; moderate confidence.

Values read '22 1' in pounds/shillings. Pence column shows a very small '0' or blank with a fractional loop that resembles three farthings (0.75). The pence-digit is uncertain but the fractional mark is present; lower confidence.
Pounds and shillings '23 6' clear. Pence reads '5' with a small loop indicating three farthings (0.75). The fraction mark and main digits align with columns; moderate confidence.
Reads '11 0 2½' — pence '2' with a fractional loop interpreted as 'ob' (halfpenny) => 0.5. Fraction symbol is distinct and typical for halfpence.
Reads '34 1 2' clearly. Description and amount alignment are legible and unambiguous.
Amount read as '1 12 11'. The '12' in shillings is clear but composition of the first digit '1' in pounds is small and could be mistaken; chosen based on alignment and spacing.
Reads '11 0 6' clearly in the pounds/shillings/pence columns. Good contrast and alignment.
